expectations.text: Routes to `Table().delete_orphans(dry_run=True)` (helpers.py:167) for orphaned DJ rows, AND `AnalysisNwbfile().cleanup(dry_run=False)` (common_nwbfile.py:754) for orphaned files on disk — these cover the two senses of 'orphans' (rows vs files)
expectations.evidence: Routes both: '(1) Orphaned table rows... delete_orphans()... (2) Orphaned .nwb files on disk... AnalysisNwbfile().cleanup()'.

expectations.text: Emphasizes the `dry_run=True` gate as a destructive-operations safety primitive — do NOT recommend running with `dry_run=False` without preview, regardless of which cleanup helper is used
expectations.evidence: Section 'Safety note': 'Always run with dry_run=True first and read the output' and 'Never run a fresh-from-memory dry_run=False call.'
